name: 1979 Iranian Constitutional Referendum
description: The constitutional referendum held on 2–3 December 1979 submitted the draft constitution produced by the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) to a popular vote. The constitution, which embedded the doctrine of velayat-e faqih and granted sweeping powers to a Supreme Leader, was approved with 99.5% of votes cast. Turnout was 75.2% (15.7 million voters). The referendum was boycotted by leftist parties, secular nationalists, Kurdish autonomy movements including the [Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/democratic-party-of-iranian-kurdistan), followers of Ayatollah Shariatmadari, and the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq). Turnout was notably low in Kurdistan, Sistan-Baluchestan, and Azerbaijan provinces.
editorial_notes: 15,690,142 voters. Timing: held during Iran hostage crisis (began 4 November 1979), which may have boosted nationalist sentiment and turnout.
